Verification:

Accepted scope:

title: Establish bead-driven agent workflow skills
description:
Objective:
Establish the repository-local quality gates, focused agent skills, and concise repository instructions required to execute the lifecycle in docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md with Codex and Gemini.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: complete lifecycle and role boundaries.
- docs/testing_and_verification_policy.md: verification layers and gate classes.
- All other files in docs/: authoritative product and architecture specifications consulted by role skills as applicable.

Scope:
Track the coherent delivery sequence for workflow conventions, quality gates, six role skills, and final AGENTS.md reconciliation.

Non-goals:
No Claude-specific setup, plugin packaging, concurrent-agent reservation system, CI provider configuration, or product implementation.

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
close_reason: Completed initial bead-driven agent workflow scaffolding.

title: Define portable skill and bead workflow conventions
description:
Objective:
Define and implement the shared repository conventions used by all bead lifecycle skills.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: bead contract, lifecycle, evidence, and handoff semantics.
- docs/testing_and_verification_policy.md: verification vocabulary and runtime classes.

Obligations:
- O1: Establish .agents/skills as the canonical repository-local Agent Skills location discoverable by Codex and Gemini.
- O2: Define the native br status plus mutually exclusive workflow label mapping for Draft, Bead Review, Ready, blocked states, implementation phases, Final Review, and Closed.
- O3: Define the bead field template: locked contract in description and acceptance criteria, supporting rationale in design, and evolving evidence and findings in notes.
- O4: Define expected change surface as advisory coordination evidence for sequential work, not a lock or permission boundary.
- O5: Provide deterministic validation where practical for required sections, status-label consistency, and exactly one active workflow label.

Forbidden outcomes:
- Do not add Claude-specific discovery paths or metadata.
- Do not create a vendor-specific plugin.
- Do not duplicate authoritative specifications into skill folders.
- Do not treat arbitrary file lists as concurrency-safe reservations.

Scope:
Shared conventions, portable skill discovery, templates, and validation needed by later role skills.

Non-goals:
No role skill implementation, final quality gate, AGENTS.md rewrite, or real parallel reservation mechanism.

Concern flags:
- Cross-agent discovery
- br lifecycle semantics
- Durable evidence format

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/
- workflow validation scripts or configuration
- .beads metadata only as required by this bead

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- O1: Codex and Gemini discovery are verified against the same canonical skill tree.
- O2: Representative lifecycle transitions are exercised with br and preserve br ready behavior for Ready work.
- O3: A representative bead passes the documented template and evidence rules.
- O4: Documentation clearly distinguishes expected change surface from reservation.
- O5: Positive and negative validator cases demonstrate concise diagnostics and nonzero failure.

Completion criteria:
Later role-skill beads can reference one stable convention without inventing status, label, discovery, or evidence behavior.
close_reason: Established canonical Codex and Gemini skill layout and bead workflow conventions.

title: Implement quiet development and final quality gates
description:
Objective:
Replace the provisional check composition with truthful, quiet development and final acceptance gates over every verification mechanism currently applicable to the repository.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/testing_and_verification_policy.md: per-change, extended, scheduled, fuzzing, and runtime-class requirements.
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: mandatory gate and acceptance semantics.

Obligations:
- O1: Provide individual Make targets for each real check and composite make check-dev and make check-final targets.
- O2: check-dev runs a very fast subset suitable for active implementation and prints one concise OK result on success.
- O3: check-final runs every finite repository-mandated acceptance check currently applicable, including strict formatting, compilation, linting, tests, documentation, dependency policy, architecture validation, and bounded fuzz or corpus checks when those facilities exist.
- O4: Successful subcommands remain quiet; failure reports the check name, command, captured diagnostics, and nonzero status.
- O5: Missing mandatory infrastructure is never reported as success. Future targets are excluded until applicable or fail explicitly if exposed as required.
- O6: Define a finite fuzz-check boundary: deterministic corpus replay or bounded smoke checks in the final gate, with long campaigns classified as extended or bead-specific verification.
- O7: Provide acceptance-record-check for the post-close, post-flush bead state without rerunning expensive Rust checks.
- O8: Keep any pre-commit hook a thin optional wrapper around authoritative Make targets rather than a second implementation.

Forbidden outcomes:
- No silent placeholder success.
- No warning output that requires subjective acceptance.
- No indefinite fuzz campaign in a commit gate.
- No weakening checks merely to clear the gate.

Scope:
Makefile check interface, quiet runner, current applicable checks, and focused verification of runner behavior.

Non-goals:
No CI provider setup, long scheduled campaigns, product tests that have no implementation yet, or automatic hook installation without an explicit repository decision.

Expected change surface:
- Makefile
- scripts/
- focused runner tests or fixtures

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- O1-O4: success and injected-failure runs prove composite coverage and output contracts.
- O5: an unavailable required command blocks rather than passing.
- O6: fuzz checks are finite and reproducible.
- O7: representative valid and invalid final bead records exercise acceptance-record-check.
- Run focused checks for the runner, then the exact final gate produced by this bead.

Completion criteria:
Implementers have one fast truthful gate, final reviewers have one comprehensive finite gate, and final bead-state validation can run after closure and flush.
close_reason: Implemented quiet development, comprehensive final, and post-close acceptance-record checks.

title: Create the author-bead skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill that derives one bounded draft implementation contract from authoritative docs.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: bead author responsibilities and approved-contract shape.
- All files in docs/: authoritative source set to search and cite as applicable.

Obligations:
- O1: Trigger on requests to author or materially revise a draft implementation bead.
- O2: Require objective, directly governing and adjacent sources, obligations, forbidden outcomes, scope, non-goals, evidence, concern flags, dependencies, expected change surface, and an explicitly unapproved review section.
- O3: Search docs with sufficient context and summarize rather than copy large specification passages.
- O4: Detect unresolved architecture, semantics, contradiction, or incoherent scope and report the proper blocker instead of inventing an answer.
- O5: Create only Draft work and never self-approve.
- O6: Sync bead changes after mutation.

Forbidden outcomes:
No self-approval, implementation, speculative architecture, compatibility shims, or pseudocode-level overprescription.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/author-bead/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A realistic authoring prompt produces a structurally valid Draft bead grounded in docs.
- A deliberately ambiguous prompt produces a precise design blocker rather than invented obligations.
- Skill validation passes and Codex and Gemini can discover the skill.

Completion criteria:
A fresh agent can author a bounded, auditable, visibly unapproved bead without additional process prompting.
close_reason: Created and validated the author-bead skill.

title: Create the review-bead skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill for independent auditing and direct correction of a Draft bead.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: bead review, convergence, and approval rules.
- All files in docs/: authoritative comparison set as applicable.

Obligations:
- O1: Audit the complete bead against governing and adjacent docs.
- O2: Directly correct resolvable omissions, distortions, weak evidence, forbidden fallbacks, and accidental scope.
- O3: Distinguish blocking findings from advisories and preserve persistent finding identities.
- O4: Converge on exactly Ready, Blocked Design, or Blocked Rescoping.
- O5: Verify the final corrected bead before approval and preserve locked-contract semantics.
- O6: Sync bead mutations and leave br status and workflow label consistent.

Forbidden outcomes:
No generic changes-requested relay, endless refinement, implementation work, or invented design decision.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/review-bead/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A flawed but resolvable draft is corrected and marked Ready.
- A semantically undecided draft is blocked for design with the exact missing decision.
- Advisory-only improvements do not prevent readiness.
- Skill validation and Codex and Gemini discovery pass.

Completion criteria:
A fresh reviewer can produce a correct executable contract or a precise blocker without relying on the author.
close_reason: Created and validated the review-bead skill.

title: Create the implement-bead skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill for implementing an approved Ready bead as an uncommitted working-tree proposal on main.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: baseline, implementation, safety, and handoff rules.
- The approved bead and all applicable files in docs/.

Obligations:
- O1: Verify Ready approval, dependencies, main, HEAD, complete status, active work, and expected change surface before editing.
- O2: Read the complete bead, governing docs, relevant types, call sites, and testing obligations.
- O3: Implement the smallest coherent approved slice with tests and provisional obligation evidence.
- O4: Run focused checks plus make check-dev while preserving additional bead-specific verification.
- O5: Leave HEAD unchanged, all implementation uncommitted, and the bead in Implementation Review.
- O6: Stop and return to review or design when implementation requires changing the locked contract.

Forbidden outcomes:
No intermediate implementation commit, self-acceptance, destructive cleanup of unexplained work, architectural invention, or silent failure.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/implement-bead/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A representative Ready bead yields an uncommitted implementation handoff with accurate evidence and check results.
- Unsafe dirty-tree and contract-ambiguity scenarios stop with precise blockers.
- Skill validation and Codex and Gemini discovery pass.

Completion criteria:
A fresh implementer can safely produce a complete reviewable working tree without committing or changing approved semantics.
close_reason: Created and validated the implement-bead skill.

title: Create the review-implementation skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill for independently reviewing and directly correcting an uncommitted bead implementation.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: implementation-review object, correction authority, and handoff.
- The approved bead and all applicable files in docs/.

Obligations:
- O1: Inspect HEAD, staged, unstaged, relevant untracked files, and surrounding unchanged code.
- O2: Perform the three-way comparison among docs, approved bead, and implementation plus tests.
- O3: Verify every obligation, negative constraint, relevant path, enforcement layer, adjacent contract, and test sensitivity.
- O4: Directly correct in-scope defects, strengthen tests, update evidence, and retain material finding identities.
- O5: Run focused verification and make check-dev after corrections.
- O6: Move corrected work to Final Review or return a materially wrong contract to bead review without silently broadening it.

Forbidden outcomes:
No comment-relay ceremony, diff-only review, hidden contract changes, commit, closure, or destructive cleanup.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/review-implementation/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A seeded implementation defect is detected, corrected directly, verified, and recorded.
- A seeded bead-contract defect returns the bead to review instead of changing scope silently.
- Skill validation and Codex and Gemini discovery pass.

Completion criteria:
A fresh reviewer can turn proposed work into a corrected Final Review candidate or identify the correct upstream blocker.
close_reason: Created and validated the review-implementation skill.

title: Create the finalize-bead skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill that independently accepts, closes, flushes, stages, and commits one completed bead.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: final review, quality gate, closure, synchronization, and commit semantics.
- docs/testing_and_verification_policy.md: applicable comprehensive and bead-specific verification.
- The approved bead and all applicable files in docs/.

Obligations:
- O1: Independently inspect the complete working tree and verify all changes belong to the active Final Review bead.
- O2: Recheck every obligation, recorded finding, evidence entry, governing constraint, and bead-specific command.
- O3: Directly correct remaining in-scope defects, then re-review and rerun affected checks.
- O4: Run make check-final against the exact accepted code tree and treat warnings, unavailable commands, hangs, flakes, and failures as blocking.
- O5: Close the bead, run br sync --flush-only, inspect bead-store changes, and run acceptance-record-check.
- O6: Stage the complete accepted change, inspect the staged diff, create one bead-prefixed semantic commit, and verify no bead-related tracked residue.
- O7: Return to bead review when acceptance requires changing the locked contract.

Forbidden outcomes:
No premature commit, post-commit closure commit, omitted mandatory check, unrelated staged work, self-referential hash, or generic commit subject.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/finalize-bead/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A representative Final Review tree follows the exact review, gate, close, flush, validation, stage, inspect, and commit order.
- Gate, sync, unrelated-change, and contract-change failures prevent commitment with precise diagnostics.
- The accepted commit contains implementation and closed synchronized bead state and begins with the bead ID.
- Skill validation and Codex and Gemini discovery pass.

Completion criteria:
A fresh final reviewer can create exactly one defensible accepted commit or stop at the correct blocker.
close_reason: Created and validated the finalize-bead skill.

title: Create the audit-committed-bead skill
description:
Objective:
Create a focused Agent Skill for retrospective semantic audit of already accepted bead-backed commits.

Authoritative sources:
- docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md: committed-code audit and corrective bead semantics.
- The original bead, accepted commit or range, and current applicable files in docs/.

Obligations:
- O1: Compare accepted code, original bead, commit history, and current authoritative docs.
- O2: Distinguish original acceptance context from later specification evolution.
- O3: Produce only no correction required, a new corrective Draft bead, or design escalation.
- O4: A corrective bead references the original bead, accepted commit, violated requirements, observed defect, and required evidence.
- O5: Preserve the original bead as historical and sync any newly created corrective bead.

Forbidden outcomes:
No silent rewrite of a closed bead, immediate code modification, reopening accepted work as a substitute for correction, or invented resolution of ambiguity.

Expected change surface:
- .agents/skills/audit-committed-bead/

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- A clean historical case reports no corrective work.
- A seeded substantive defect creates a correctly linked Draft corrective bead.
- A seeded specification ambiguity escalates design without proposing invented code.
- Skill validation and Codex and Gemini discovery pass.

Completion criteria:
A fresh auditor can inspect accepted history without corrupting its record or bypassing the ordinary lifecycle.
close_reason: Created and validated the audit-committed-bead skill.

title: Replace provisional AGENTS.md with mossignal workflow guidance
description:
Objective:
Replace the copied provisional AGENTS.md with concise mossignal-specific repository guidance that exposes the finished skill workflow and authoritative commands to Codex and Gemini.

Authoritative sources:
- All files in docs/, with docs/bead_review_implementation_commit_process.md governing lifecycle and docs/testing_and_verification_policy.md governing verification.
- The implemented role skills and Make targets created by predecessor beads.

Obligations:
- O1: Remove copied game-engine assumptions and describe only mossignal repository rules.
- O2: Name the six focused skills and explain which user-assigned role task triggers each one.
- O3: State that all docs are authoritative until a later explicit precedence policy exists.
- O4: Document main-only uncommitted implementation, sequential code-changing agents, dirty-tree safety, and no architectural invention.
- O5: Document make check-dev, make check-final, acceptance-record-check, and bead-specific verification responsibilities accurately.
- O6: Keep durable global constraints in AGENTS.md and leave detailed role procedures in on-demand skills.

Forbidden outcomes:
No retention of copied domain rules, duplication of complete skill bodies, Claude-specific setup, or instructions that conflict with the process specification.

Expected change surface:
- AGENTS.md

Bead review:
Status: Unreviewed
Findings: None recorded
Readiness verdict: Not approved
acceptance_criteria:
Required evidence:
- AGENTS.md contains no copied game-engine terminology or obsolete commands.
- Every referenced skill and Make target exists and matches its actual behavior.
- A cold Codex and Gemini session can discover the workflow entry points without loading every detailed procedure eagerly.
- Documentation and final repository gates pass.

Completion criteria:
The default repository instructions are concise, mossignal-specific, and correctly route agents into the reviewed skills.
close_reason: Replaced provisional repository guidance with mossignal-specific Codex and Gemini instructions.

title: Implement problem and report kernel
description:
# Objective

Implement Premium Foundation Roadmap item 7 as the durable common problem-reporting architecture consumed by the opening authored-graph and structural-validation tasks. Do not introduce a temporary diagnostic or identity model.

## Contract basis

All four governing records are reviewed and accepted for reuse at their recorded evidence baselines:

- `mossignal.diagnostics.subject_references`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-subject-references.yaml`
  - role: stable diagnostic subject identity, related-subject roles, and canonical ordering for the seven initial variants
- `mossignal.diagnostics.problem_model`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-problem-model.yaml`
  - role: catalogue registry, codes, classifications, typed evidence, `Problem<D>`, and `Diagnostic<D>`
- `mossignal.diagnostics.collections`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-collections.yaml`
  - role: `DiagnosticSet<D>`, canonical ordering, condition identity, deduplication, and evidence merging for the opening slice
- `mossignal.diagnostics.artifact_reports`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/artifact-reports.yaml`
  - role: `Report<T, D>`, artifact blocking semantics, and complete diagnostic retention

## Included scope

- Implement the durable catalogue-backed diagnostic kernel.
- Implement the specified `Severity` and `Responsibility` families with code-fixed lookup.
- Implement `Problem<D>`, typed code-specific `ProblemEvidence<D>`, related subjects, safe suggestions, and the ReportFinding-only `Diagnostic<D>` wrapper.
- Implement `SubjectRef` support needed by this slice for `Network`, `Node`, `InPort`, `OutPort`, `Connection`, `ExternalInput`, and `ExternalOutput`, including their permanent comparison tags and canonical payload order.
- Implement only these opening structural-validation codes: `validation.duplicate_key`, `validation.missing_node`, `validation.missing_port`, `validation.missing_endpoint`, `validation.invalid_direction`, `validation.signal_kind_mismatch`, `validation.unsupported_multiple_drivers`, `validation.missing_required_input`, and `validation.invalid_fixed_arity`.
- Implement `DiagnosticSet<D>` with canonical iteration, condition-key deduplication, and the specified exact-agreement, set-union, and duplicate-claim multiset behavior.
- Implement `Report<T, D>` and `ReportFailure<D>` with complete diagnostic retention and blocking-artifact behavior.
- Use one authoritative declarative registry for every implemented code; do not maintain parallel code, evidence, classification, delivery, discriminator, or merge tables.

## Explicit exclusions

- `SubjectRef` variants and payload comparison outside the seven initial variants.
- Exhaustive catalogue implementation beyond the nine opening validation codes.
- Module-binding, reassociation, and module-interface diagnostic identity contexts.
- Authored graph storage and structural validation detector logic.
- Runtime diagnostic occurrences.
- Persistent diagnostic episodes.
- Internal-defect delivery infrastructure beyond the representation needed for `internal.diagnostic_evidence_conflict`.
- Provenance, rendering, localization, logging, observer delivery, persistence encoding, serialization, and schema decoding.
- Any operation for combining two `Report` values or resolving two produced artifacts.
- Rust compatibility shims or a second temporary problem representation.

These exclusions correspond to adjacent or `known_uncovered` contract facets and do not block this bead.

## Implementation freedom

- Private enum and struct names, role and category variant spelling, storage layout, helper types, construction APIs, comparison keys, and merge algorithms remain implementation choices when the specified distinctions and deterministic behavior are preserved.
- A private diagnostic projection may preserve authored facts needed by evidence without exposing complete authored record storage shapes.
- Canonical subject comparison may use a private comparator or ordering key; no public `Ord` implementation is required by this bead.
- Implementation may create or extend draft contracts when it discovers reusable specification-backed knowledge already determined by the specifications and this bead. Such drafts require independent review before final acceptance and do not authorize scope expansion.

## Expected change surface

- A diagnostics-focused module or small module family under `crates/mossignal/src/`.
- Public module integration in `crates/mossignal/src/lib.rs`.
- Focused unit and compile-boundary documentation tests colocated with the implementation.
- No unrelated changes to signal, time, key, or metadata semantics.

## Required verification

- Registry coverage and exact fixed severity, responsibility, delivery, evidence association, primary-subject rule, discriminator, and merge law for every implemented code.
- Safe APIs cannot construct code/evidence, classification, or delivery mismatches.
- Structured comparison excludes rendered prose.
- Stable ordering across the seven initial `SubjectRef` variants, with tag comparison before payload comparison and `Level` before `Pulse` for erased signal-bearing keys.
- Permutations of discovery and unordered evidence produce identical `DiagnosticSet` values and iteration order.
- Duplicate-key evidence is independent of discovery order, symmetric, complete, canonically ordered, and retains meaningful claim multiplicity without multiplying repeated detection.
- Deduplication covers repeated equal exact-agreement evidence, distinct subjects, distinct discriminators, partial conflicting-driver set union, partial fixed-arity port-set union, and contradictory exact-agreement evidence.
- Contradictory exact-agreement evidence produces `internal.diagnostic_evidence_conflict`.
- Report tests cover blocking artifact absence, non-blocking artifact presence, complete `ReportFailure` diagnostics, and accumulation of unrelated safe findings.
- Run focused tests while iterating, then `make check-dev` and `make check-final`.

## Blocking open questions

None.

## Readiness

Open and claimable. The required stable key families already exist, the represented opening semantics are reviewed, and this bead has no dependency on the contracts' known-uncovered future facets.
close_reason: Corrected diagnostic condition identity, merging, conflict retention, and regression coverage

Accepted scope:

title: Implement restricted authored-graph structural validation
description:
## Objective

Implement Premium Foundation Roadmap item 9: the structural validation phase for the restricted level-only `Constant`/`Not` authored graph. It must report all independently discoverable structural defects deterministically and produce only the private candidate consumed by roadmap item 10.

## Contract basis

- `mossignal.graph.authored_network_definition`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/authored-network-definition.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, used unchanged
  - role: lossless unchecked graph records, stable keys, endpoint categories, malformed-claim retention, and restricted node shapes.

- `mossignal.diagnostics.subject_references`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-subject-references.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, used unchanged
  - role: stable diagnostic subjects and initial canonical subject ordering.

- `mossignal.diagnostics.problem_model`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-problem-model.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, used unchanged
  - role: opening validation codes, fixed classifications, typed evidence, and problem construction.

- `mossignal.diagnostics.collections`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/diagnostic-collections.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, used unchanged
  - role: canonical order, condition identity, evidence merge laws, and opening structural-validation diagnostic behavior.

- `mossignal.diagnostics.artifact_reports`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/artifact-reports.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, used unchanged
  - role: artifact absence under blocking findings and safe report accumulation.

- `mossignal.graph.structural_validation`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/structural-validation.yaml`
  - status: draft, created by preparation
  - role: task-specific reusable boundary for restricted structural validation and its private pre-semantic candidate.

## Included scope

- Add the structural validation entry point for `UncheckedNetwork<D>` and the internal validation workspace/candidate it needs.
- Detect duplicate stable keys in the opening node, input-port, output-port, connection, external-input, and external-output scopes without losing conflicting authored claims.
- Validate connection endpoint existence and incidence, source/destination direction, and signal-kind compatibility.
- Enforce the opening single-driver input policy while preserving intrinsic fan-out.
- Validate fixed port groups for the restricted shapes: Constant has zero inputs and one level output; Not has one level input and one level output. Missing required Not input must be diagnosed separately from malformed port-group arity.
- Validate every external-output source reference as a structural source endpoint.
- Emit only the applicable opening blocking `validation.*` conditions with the existing diagnostic kernel's canonical ordering, condition identity, and evidence merge semantics.
- Collect independent safe findings and produce no public artifact when any blocking error remains.
- On structural success, retain only private structurally usable data required by the reaction-dependency extraction task.

## Exclusions and non-goals

- Do not extract reaction dependencies, inspect SCCs, detect reaction cycles, define topological order, or construct `ValidatedNetwork`.
- Do not add dense indices, compiled descriptors, evaluator behavior, machine state, input artifacts, modules, hierarchy, persistence, or topology patches.
- Do not extend the diagnostic catalogue or create non-blocking quality diagnostics for this slice.
- Do not discard, repair, default, or panic on malformed unchecked data.
- Do not expose a new public lifecycle stage or public validation-candidate type.

## Implementation obligations

- Respect every required facet of `mossignal.graph.structural_validation`, especially rules `validates-restricted-structural-invariants`, `reports-opening-blocking-conditions`, `accumulates-safe-independent-findings`, `preserves-stable-subjects`, and `candidate-is-private-and-pre-semantic`.
- Keep diagnostic primary subjects, discriminators, and evidence aligned with the reviewed opening diagnostic contracts; stable keys and subject references, not traversal or storage positions, are authoritative.
- Treat malformed definitions as caller-controlled data and return structured findings without panicking.
- Preserve enough lossless association to continue checking unrelated defects safely after individual malformed claims.
- Keep candidate construction private to crate internals; it must not imply that structural validation alone satisfies the public validated-network lifecycle.

## Allowed implementation freedom

- Private candidate type, internal storage, pass decomposition, map choice, and helper names are free.
- Multiple deterministic validation passes are permitted.
- The implementation may retain private stable-keyed lookup views for later task 10, but may not introduce dense compiled indices.
- Use concise `SPEC:` comments only for non-obvious safeguards at the public-lifecycle or malformed-claim boundaries.

## Expected change surface

- `crates/mossignal/src/authored.rs`
- `crates/mossignal/src/diagnostics.rs` only where the existing opening diagnostic kernel needs a specification-backed correction to support the already-reviewed laws
- `crates/mossignal/src/lib.rs`
- one new private validation module if a separate module clarifies the boundary
- focused unit tests alongside the relevant modules
- `docs/specs/contracts/structural-validation.yaml` only for implementation-discovered, reusable specification facts; any change remains draft
- `.beads/` when implementation acceptance closes the bead

## Required verification

- Start each defect-class correction with a focused failing regression test and observe the failure before changing production behavior.
- Add at least one focused valid and invalid case for each included structural rule.
- Verify independent malformed conditions accumulate where safe, blocking reports omit artifacts, diagnostics are structurally asserted, and output is invariant under permutations of unordered authored claims.
- Run focused Rust tests while iterating, then `make check-dev`; final acceptance additionally runs `make check-final`.

## Dependencies

- Roadmap item 8 / `ms-unchecked-authored-graph-wsj` is complete.
- Roadmap item 10 depends on this bead's private candidate boundary and must not be folded into it.

## Readiness

Draft — requires independent contract and bead review. No unresolved observable product question blocks the stated slice; the only intentional deferral is reaction-dependency semantics owned by item 10.

title: Implement unchecked authored graph representation
description:
## Objective

Implement premium foundation roadmap item 8: a deliberately restricted, authoritative `UncheckedNetwork<D>` representation for level-only `Constant` and `Not` definitions. It must retain malformed authored structure for the following validation task rather than repairing or rejecting it during construction.

## Contract basis

- `mossignal.graph.authored_network_definition`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/authored-network-definition.yaml`
  - status: reviewed (accepted by independent review)
  - required facets: authoritative stable-keyed structural graph; owned unchecked construction; typed and erased endpoint forms; retention of malformed claims; metadata attachment; Constant/Not shapes; external boundaries; deferred validation transition.

- `mossignal.diagnostics.artifact_reports`
  - `docs/specs/contracts/artifact-reports.yaml`
  - status: reviewed, contextual only
  - role: governs the later validation report, which this task does not implement.

## Included scope

- Add the authored graph module and export its deliberate public unchecked-definition types.
- Represent network identity, external level inputs and outputs, `NodeDef<D>`, `NodeKind<D>` restricted to `Constant` and `Not`, typed input/output ports, connections, and `DiagnosticMeta`.
- Use existing stable typed/erased key types to retain signal kind in endpoints while allowing heterogeneous collections.
- Provide direct, safe construction suitable for deserializers, editors, generated definitions, fuzzing, and test fixtures.
- Preserve all supplied structural claims losslessly, including duplicate keys, dangling references, mismatched kinds, invalid directions, conflicting drivers, and absent fixed ports; duplicate-key storage must not overwrite earlier claims.
- Add focused representation tests covering a valid restricted definition, both level and pulse endpoint representation, metadata attachment, direct malformed construction, and duplicate-key retention.

## Exclusions and non-goals

- No structural validation, diagnostic emission, `Report` production, private validation candidate, reaction dependencies, cycle checking, `ValidatedNetwork`, dense indices, compilation, machine/runtime execution, modules, persistence encoding, or topology patches.
- Do not add placeholder semantics for node kinds beyond `Constant` and `Not`.
- Do not make constructor-time validity checks that discard or rewrite malformed authored input.

## Implementation obligations

- Keep the representation authoritative and stable-keyed; no dense runtime positions or evaluator scheduling belong here.
- Model external endpoints as network boundaries rather than nodes.
- Constant definitions must record their `LogicLevel` configuration and level output shape; Not definitions must record their one level input and level output shape.
- Preserve signal kind in all typed endpoints; erasure is allowed only for heterogeneous storage and must retain the kind.
- Keep the public API owned and non-callback-based.
- Add concise `SPEC:` comments only where a non-obvious lossless-storage choice prevents weakening the malformed-input boundary.

## Allowed implementation freedom

- Private collection layout is free provided it losslessly preserves duplicate and malformed claims.
- Exact constructor/helper naming and module-internal layout are free.
- The representation may use simple vectors as the canonical opening storage.

## Expected change surface

- `crates/mossignal/src/lib.rs`
- new authored-graph source module and its focused unit tests
- `docs/specs/contracts/authored-network-definition.yaml` only if implementation discovers additional specification-backed facts (any such change remains draft)
- `.beads/` state when the implementation is accepted

## Required verification

- Run focused authored-graph tests while iterating.
- Run `make check-dev` before completion and `make check-final` for final acceptance.
- Do not claim validation behavior; the next roadmap item owns validation-focused positive/negative conformance cases.

## Readiness

Accepted for implementation. No unresolved observable product question blocks this bounded slice.
notes: Independent review accepted the contract and bounded implementation scope. The reviewed contract requires an unchecked endpoint-claim form that preserves endpoint category and signal kind even for direction-invalid connections; exact public spelling and storage remain implementation freedom.
close_reason: Implemented unchecked authored network representation with lossless malformed claims and focused tests.
